vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a DSG dual-clutch transmission — it requires 12V power to shift reliably.
Locate the shift lock slot — Cap near selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.

DSG REQUIRES POWER: Battery is under the rear seat — but use the jump terminals UNDER THE HOOD to connect your jump pack. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If mechanical slot is used without power: DSG transmissions may resist — apply steady firm pressure and hold for several seconds.
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N. DSG — restore power first.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: Under hood or under rear seat — varies by generation
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals. AGM jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a DSG dual-clutch transmission — it requires 12V power to shift reliably.
Locate the shift lock slot — Cap near selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.

DSG REQUIRES POWER: Battery is under the rear seat — but use the jump terminals UNDER THE HOOD to connect your jump pack. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If mechanical slot is used without power: DSG transmissions may resist — apply steady firm pressure and hold for several seconds.
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N. DSG: restore power first. Golf R AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD/GTI: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) on ground. Golf R 4MOTION AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

knownIssues.title: Golf R AWD Cannot Be Disabled — Flatbed Only
knownIssues.detail: The Golf R 4MOTION AWD system cannot be disabled. Golf R must always be flatbedded. Standard Golf and GTI are FWD and can be wheel-lifted normally from the rear.

knownIssues.title: DSG Requires Power to Shift — Battery Often Under Rear Seat
knownIssues.detail: DSG dual-clutch transmission requires 12V power to shift to neutral. Battery is often under the rear seat on Golf variants — not always under the hood. Locate battery before attempting to jump.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals — NOT rear seat battery directly. AGM jump pack. Golf R: flatbed only (4MOTION AWD).

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot near selector base on console.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. 4MOTION AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

battery.location: Under hood or under rear seat
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn the Volkswagen ID.4 / ID.Buzz to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (12V auxiliary). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY — Electric Drivetrain
knownIssues.detail: Electric RWD or AWD. Towing with wheels on ground will damage motors. Flatbed only with no exceptions.

knownIssues.title: 12V Battery Under Hood
knownIssues.detail: Unlike some EVs, the ID.4 and ID.Buzz 12V auxiliary battery is located under the conventional hood area. Standard jump procedure applies.

battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV battery. Flatbed only.
